page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 48 vCPUs, consisting of 24 physical cores with hyper-threading, and 180 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results show strong performance in compute-heavy workloads. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core execution, image processing, and text processing. It also demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, scoring 18084.0 in Geekbench multi-core and 54598.51 in Passmark CPU tests. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform well, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 34.08 GB/sec and Passmark encryption scoring 43744.82. Memory bandwidth is measured at 144.84 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

The inclusion of Intel AMX enables efficient on-CPU AI workloads, as demonstrated by a strong prompt processing speed of 198.07 tokens/sec for medium 7B LLM models, though larger 70B models run at a more moderate 2.81 tokens/sec for text generation. Given the lack of local storage and GPU accelerators, this server is best suited for compute-intensive applications, software compilation, database operations, and web serving rather than massive-scale deep learning training or storage-heavy workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a compute-focused x86_64 server featuring 48 vCPUs and 180 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on its 24 physical cores and advanced instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, to deliver strong performance. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core execution, image processing, and text processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and database operations. It is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, though it is less suited for storage-heavy or GPU-dependent tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 features 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong compute and database performance.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon server with advanced matrix extensions, delivering strong compute performance for compilation, database, and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Delivers top-tier single-core performance, image processing, and text processing in benchmarks.
Strong multi-core capabilities for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration.
